codeReview.summary: The backend now exposes Claude row metadata and row-specific detail resolution, but the change is not backward-compatible with the committed callers. For duplicate Claude skills, `get_skill_detail` now errors unless `row_id` is supplied, while the committed platform/detail UI still opens detail by plain `skillId` only. That breaks the mission-critical duplicate-row list-to-detail flow instead of enabling it.

issues.description: `get_claude_observation_detail` now rejects duplicate Claude detail requests with `Multiple Claude rows found ...; row_id is required`, but this feature does not update any committed consumer to send `row_id` or route detail by row identity. In commit `2eae38e`, `src/pages/PlatformView.tsx:177` still opens detail with `handleOpenDrawer(skill.id)`, `src/components/skill/SkillDetailView.tsx:281-283` still loads by `skillId`, and `src/stores/skillDetailStore.ts:163-165` still invokes `get_skill_detail` with only `{ skillId }` (plus `read_skill_content` by `skillId`). Result: when the same Claude skill exists in user and marketplace roots, opening either row now fails instead of preserving the selected source-specific detail state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend worker skill procedure is missing the frontend-targeted validation requirement for backend payload changes. It only mandates Rust-targeted checks, but this mission's shared guidance requires frontend-targeted tests/typecheck/lint too when IPC payloads change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/claude-scan-backend-worker/SKILL.md:34-37` lists only `test-rust-targeted` and `clippy`, while `.factory/services.yaml:3-8` and mission `AGENTS.md:37-43` also require `test-frontend-targeted`, `typecheck`, and `lint`. The worker handoff's verification section shows they had to run `pnpm exec vitest run src/test/platformStore.test.ts src/test/PlatformView.test.tsx src/test/SkillDetailView.test.tsx src/test/skillDetailStore.test.ts && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint` anyway.

summary: Review failed. The feature correctly adds Claude row metadata, but it introduces a blocking compatibility regression: duplicate Claude detail requests now require `row_id` even though the committed UI/store callers still request detail by `skillId` only. I also noted a shared-state gap in the backend worker skill, which understates the required frontend-side validation for IPC contract changes.
